summary: base58-core ships powershell exec + child_process + HTTP POST patterns in dist/index.* (likely credential stealer)
details: Package `base58-core@1.0.1` presents itself as a base58 encoding utility but its compiled `dist/index.js` and `dist/index.cjs` contain a host-reconnaissance and exfiltration code shape inconsistent with that declared purpose: `require('child_process')` is imported twice and used to invoke `execSync('powershell...')`, alongside hostname/id collection and HTTP POST primitives in the same module. A base58 codec has no legitimate need to spawn PowerShell, enumerate host identifiers, or open outbound HTTP connections. The combination of PowerShell execution plus host-info collection plus POST in a small dependency module matches the fingerprint of installer-side host reconnaissance and data exfiltration to an attacker-controlled endpoint, and is reachable as soon as a consumer requires the module.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
